titel: Sampling of data on household debt.
html:
It is important for both individuals and economic development that households have the opportunity to obtain credit for consumption and home purchases. At the same time, high levels of debt can create vulnerability for individuals, the financial system, and the overall economy. For individual households, for example, high interest and repayment payments in relation to income can lead to increased vulnerability in the event of negative life events. To understand these risks at both the individual and system level, reliable statistics are needed. However, information about household finances is sensitive. Therefore, statistics should be limited to the most necessary information about household finances.

The proposal suggests that Statistics Sweden's (SCB) microsimulation model, the distribution analysis statistical system for incomes and transfers (Fasit), should be expanded to include data on household sector debt and debt payments. By processing this data in Fasit, it enables better analysis of individuals' and households' resilience and sensitivity to economic shocks. This can contribute to the decision-making basis for economic policy and help identify appropriate measures.

The proposed amendments to the law should come into force on August 1, 2026.

Proposal for a Parliamentary Decision

Prop. 2025/26:255

Government Proposal: The Riksdag (Swedish Parliament) adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2019:508) concerning the processing of personal data in the distribution analysis statistics system for income and transfers. 3

Prop. 2025/26:255 2 Proposed Amendment to the Act

(2019:508) concerning the processing of personal data in the distribution analysis statistics system for income and transfers.

Hereby, with regard to the Act (2019:508) concerning the processing of personal data in the distribution analysis statistics system for income and transfers, it is stipulated that sections 1, 3, 4, 6, 8, and 9, and the heading immediately preceding section 8, shall have the following wording, and that a new section, 8a, of the following wording shall be introduced.

Current Wording Proposed Wording 1 § This Act shall apply to the processing of personal data by the Statistics Sweden (Statistiska centralbyrån) in the distribution analysis statistics system for income and transfers (Fasit), which the agency manages.

The Act shall also apply to The Act shall also apply to authorities' processing of per- authorities' processing of per- sonal data in the selection that has sonal data in the selections that have been provided according to section 9. been provided according to section 9.

This Act also contains provisions regarding data reporting obligations for certain companies.

3 § Statistics Sweden is the data controller for the processing of personal data that the agency performs according to this Act or according to regulations issued in connection with it.

Authorities to which the selection has Authorities to which the selections have been provided according to section 9 are data controllers for the processing of personal data that the authorities perform.

4 §

Personal data may be processed in Fasit and in the selection that has been provided according to section 9 if it is necessary to

  1. simulate and calculate the effects

of existing and new regulations and other changes within tax, levy, and contribution systems and public welfare services, or

4 Personal data may be processed in Fasit and in the selections that have been provided according to section 9 if it is necessary to

  1. simulate and calculate the effects of existing and new regulations and other changes

a) within tax, levy, and contribution systems and public welfare services, and b) of household sector debts Prop. 2025/26:255

and payments, or

  1. produce statistics for the analysis of
  2. produce statistics for the analysis of

information within tax, levy, information a) within tax, levy, and contribution systems and public welfare services, and b) regarding household sector debts and payments.

6 § Personal data referred to in Article 9.1 and Article 10 of the EU's General Data Protection Regulation may not be processed in Fasit or in the selection provided according to section 9.

The first paragraph does not apply to data that reveal ethnic origin or membership in a trade union, and data about health.

Provision of Collection and provision of personal data

8 § Statistics Sweden shall produce a special selection of personal data in Fasit that cannot directly be linked to a natural person for provision according to section 9. Regarding household sector debts and payments, Statistics Sweden shall only produce a smaller selection of such personal data as referred to in the first paragraph.

8a § Companies covered by the Act (2004:297) on banking and financial services and the Act (2016:1024) on mortgage lending shall provide Statistics Sweden with information on household sector debts and payments. The government may issue regulations on which information shall be provided.

9 § The selection according to section 8 may, upon request, be provided by Statistics Sweden to the Riksdag Administration and the Swedish National Audit Office, as well as to the Government Offices and other government agencies.

This Act shall enter into force on August 1, 2026. 6
tldr: The government wants to amend the law so that the Central Bureau of Statistics (SCB) can collect more information about the amount of debt held by households, as well as the interest rates and repayments they are making. This is being done to better understand economic risks in society.
lattlast:
The government has proposed expanding the functionality of a system called Fasit. Fasit is a tool used by the Central Bureau of Statistics, also known as SCB, to analyze how various economic regulations affect people. By adding information about debts and payments, it becomes possible to better understand how sensitive households are to changes in the economy.

To make this work, banks and other companies that provide mortgages must provide SCB with information about household debts and payments. It is important to know how much money is being allocated to debt repayment in order to plan the country's economic policy. This helps authorities to assess whether high interest rates or other costs are causing significant problems for families.

Despite the collection of more information, the rules regarding personal data are strict. Only data that is necessary should be used, and sensitive information such as health or ethnic origin should not be used. The goal is to obtain good statistics without infringing on people's privacy.

If the proposal is approved and the parliament votes in favor, the new rules will come into effect on August 1, 2026.
